new_classes.label_en: Semi-Sparkling Wine (1 bar ≤ excess pressure < 3 bar, CN 2204 21 06–09)
new_classes.definition_en: Wine of fresh grapes, other than sparkling wine (CN 2204 10), presented either in bottles with mushroom stoppers held in place by ties or fastenings, or otherwise put up with an excess pressure due to carbon dioxide in solution of not less than 1 bar but less than 3 bar, measured at a temperature of 20 °C, in containers holding 2 litres or less (CN subheading 2204 21).

A quality borne by a packaging individual indicating that it is a bottle closed with a mushroom-shaped stopper held in place by ties or fastenings, as required for semi-sparkling wines under CN subheadings 2204 21 06–09.

coverage_explanation: The core distinguishing criteria for CN 2204 21 06–09 are captured: (1) wine of fresh grapes other than sparkling wine (2204 10), (2) excess pressure due to dissolved CO₂ of ≥ 1 bar and < 3 bar at 20 °C, (3) presented in containers of ≤ 2 litres (subheading 2204 21 vs 2204 29), and (4) bottled with mushroom stoppers held by ties/fastenings (the alternative presentation criterion). The MushroomStopperBottleQuality class captures the stopper criterion at the packaging level. However, the exact CO₂ measurement temperature condition (20 °C) cannot be fully axiomatised in OWL-DL without a complex qualified cardinality on a temperature datatype, and the internal split within 2204 21 06–09 (which further subdivides by alcohol content and geographic designation of origin) is not captured here as those sub-criteria apply to sibling TARIC codes rather than 22042109 specifically. The disjointness from SparklingWine (≥ 3 bar) is partially expressed via the complement restriction. Coverage is therefore approximately 0.75.
